cases.title: TS-27: Second student submitting Consumed token rejected with security log entry
preconditions: Active session with two enrolled students Student A and Student B
Student A successfully checked in; QrToken status Consumed with consumedByStudentId Student A
Token still within 30-second validity window (TestClock fixed before expiry)
Student B authenticated with in-radius coordinates and Pending attendance
steps: GET /api/v1/check-in/tokens/:consumedTokenId/preflight as Student B and assert HTTP 403 errorCode TokenAlreadyUsed per API design §6.2
Assert message Mã QR đã được sử dụng per error-handling §4.4
Assert Student B attendance remains Pending (not Present)
Verify TokenReuseAlert security audit entry contains sessionId, tokenId, and submitting Student B id per BR-11 outcome and AC-10c
Optionally POST /api/v1/check-in as Student B with same tokenId and assert HTTP 400 TokenAlreadyUsed as defense-in-depth per validation-rules §4 BR-11
Query check_in_attempts for optional POST attempt and assert outcome TokenAlreadyUsed when submit path exercised
cases.expected: BR-11 enforced: consumed token reuse rejected at preflight (403) or submit (400) within validity window; append-only TokenReuseAlert security audit with session ID, token ID, and submitting student ID

cases.title: CheckInService rejects Consumed token at pipeline step 8 before duplicate and GPS checks
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL test database is running
Active session with qr_tokens row status Consumed linked to sessionId
Enrolled Student B with Pending attendance and in-radius coordinates
CheckInService wired per validation-rules §5.1 with TestClock within token TTL
steps: Invoke CheckInService.submitCheckIn with Consumed tokenId and valid locationPayload for Student B
Assert result outcome TokenAlreadyUsed without Present transition
Query attendance_records for Student B and assert status remains Pending
Query qr_tokens and assert status remains Consumed (no double-consumption)
Insert check_in_attempts row and assert outcome TokenAlreadyUsed logged
Verify pipeline short-circuits at validation-rules §5.1 step 8 before step 10 BR-04 duplicate evaluation per business rules §8 order 4
cases.expected: BR-11 enforced at Layer 4 step 8; TokenAlreadyUsed returned without consuming token again or creating Present record

cases.title: CheckInTransaction atomically sets QrToken Consumed only on Success not on TokenAlreadyUsed
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with qr_tokens row status Consumed by Student A
Enrolled Student B with Pending attendance
CheckInTransaction suite per testing-plan §5 with serializable transaction and row lock on qr_tokens
steps: Begin transaction context and invoke CheckInService.submit with Consumed tokenId for Student B
Assert outcome TokenAlreadyUsed and transaction rolled back without Present update for Student B
Assert qr_tokens row remains Consumed with consumedByStudentId still Student A
Contrast: invoke CheckInService.submit on Valid token for Student C and assert single commit sets Consumed and Present atomically
Verify domain model §5.2: at most one successful consumption per token
cases.expected: BR-11 one-time-use invariant: Present + token Consumed atomically on Success only; reuse rejected without corrupting attendance or token state

cases.title: POST /check-in returns 400 TokenAlreadyUsed envelope for Consumed token submission
preconditions: QrToken row with status Consumed linked to Active session
Authenticated enrolled Student B who did not consume the token
Token issuedAt within 30-second window (not ExpiredQr path)
Student B coordinates within session gpsRadiusMeters
steps: POST /api/v1/check-in with consumed tokenId, latitude 10.762700, longitude 106.660200 using Student B session
Assert HTTP status 400 Bad Request
Assert Content-Type application/json; charset=utf-8
Assert response body outcome TokenAlreadyUsed and errorCode TokenAlreadyUsed
Assert response body message Mã QR đã được sử dụng per validation-rules §4 BR-11 mapping
Verify CheckInAttempt row inserted with outcome TokenAlreadyUsed
cases.expected: HTTP 400 TokenAlreadyUsed contract for one-time-use token violation at submit layer; distinct from 409 DuplicateCheckIn per BR-11

cases.title: Flow B steps 3–4: Student A duplicate retry then Student B same-token reuse in anti-fraud path
preconditions: Flow B steps 1–2 complete: Active session with valid QR, Student A checked in successfully
Student A attendance Present; tokenId from step 2 status Consumed
Student B enrolled and authenticated with in-radius coordinates
TestClock within 30-second token validity window
steps: Student A GET /api/v1/check-in/tokens/:freshValidTokenId/preflight and assert HTTP 200 outcome Valid
Student A POST /api/v1/check-in with fresh Valid tokenId and assert HTTP 409 DuplicateCheckIn
Student B GET /api/v1/check-in/tokens/:consumedTokenId/preflight with same tokenId consumed in step 2
Assert HTTP 403 with errorCode TokenAlreadyUsed per API design §6.2 preflight table
Verify TokenReuseAlert security audit entry for Student B attempt with sessionId, tokenId, studentId per AC-10c
Assert Student B attendance remains Pending; GpsCaptureStep never reached per BR-15
Confirm Flow B anti-fraud rejection path per testing-plan §6 Flow B steps 3–4 tags BR-11
cases.expected: Flow B validates BR-11 token-sharing rejection at preflight after first successful consumption; TokenReuseAlert security audit created for Student B reuse attempt

cases.title: QrToken transitions Valid to Consumed only on first successful check-in not on reuse rejection
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with qr_tokens row status Valid linked to Active session
Student A enrolled with Pending attendance
Student B enrolled with Pending attendance
CheckInService.submit callable with in-radius coordinates
steps: Record beforeState: qr_token status Valid, consumedAt null
Invoke CheckInService.submit for Student A and assert Success
Assert afterState: qr_token status Consumed with consumedAt and consumedByStudentId Student A
Invoke CheckInService.submit for Student B with same tokenId
Assert outcome TokenAlreadyUsed; qr_token afterState remains Consumed (no re-transition)
Verify TokenReuseAlert security audit entry created for Student B attempt per BR-11 outcome and AC-10c
cases.expected: beforeState Valid → afterState Consumed on first Success only; reuse attempts do not alter token state but create TokenReuseAlert security audit

cases.title: Parallel check-in with same token from two students yields one Success and one TokenAlreadyUsed
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL test database with serializable or SELECT FOR UPDATE on qr_tokens
Active session with Valid QrToken T1 and two enrolled students Student A and Student B
Both students authenticated with in-radius coordinates and Pending attendance
TestClock within attendance window and token TTL
steps: Fire two parallel POST /check-in or CheckInService.submit calls using identical tokenId T1 from Student A and Student B
Collect both HTTP responses or CheckInResult outcomes
Assert exactly one response is HTTP 200 outcome Success with attendance Present
Assert the other response is HTTP 400 outcome TokenAlreadyUsed
Query qr_tokens for T1 and assert status Consumed with exactly one consumedByStudentId
Query attendance_records and assert exactly one Present row among the two students
Verify TokenReuseAlert security audit entry for the losing student's attempt per AC-10c
cases.expected: BR-11 concurrency invariant: row lock on qr_tokens ensures at most one successful consumption; loser receives TokenAlreadyUsed and TokenReuseAlert audit per NFR-02

cases.title: TokenReuseAlert security audit persisted on token reuse with sessionId, tokenId, and submitting studentId
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL test database is running
Active session with Consumed qr_tokens row consumed by Student A
Student B enrolled with Pending attendance
Security audit store queryable per error-handling §6.2 TokenReuseAlert event type
steps: Record TokenReuseAlert security audit count before reuse attempt
Invoke CheckInService.submit with Consumed tokenId for Student B
Assert outcome TokenAlreadyUsed
Query security audit store and assert new TokenReuseAlert entry contains sessionId matching token sessionId
Assert entry contains tokenId and submitting studentId Student B
Assert entry is append-only (no update or delete of prior entries) per AC-10c
cases.expected: BR-11 outcome requires append-only TokenReuseAlert security audit with session ID, token ID, and submitting student ID on every consumed-token reuse attempt

cases.title: TokenAlreadyUsed evaluated at pipeline step 8 before DuplicateCheckIn when token already Consumed
preconditions: Student A consumed token T1 and is Present for Active session
Fresh scenario: Student A attempts check-in again with same consumed tokenId T1
TestClock within 30-second validity window
Student A coordinates within session gpsRadiusMeters
steps: POST /api/v1/check-in as Student A with same consumed tokenId T1
Assert HTTP status 400 with outcome TokenAlreadyUsed (not 409 DuplicateCheckIn)
Assert errorCode TokenAlreadyUsed takes precedence per validation-rules §5.1 step 8 before step 10
Verify check_in_attempts logged with outcome TokenAlreadyUsed not DuplicateCheckIn
Query qr_tokens for T1 and assert status remains Consumed
cases.expected: BR-11 evaluated at validation-rules §5.1 step 8 before BR-04 DuplicateCheckIn (step 10); Consumed token short-circuits per business rules §8 order 4

cases.title: Consumed token reuse within 30s window returns TokenAlreadyUsed not ExpiredQr
preconditions: QrToken consumed by Student A at time T with expires_at T + 30 s
TestClock fixed at T + 20 seconds (within validity window)
Student B enrolled, authenticated, with in-radius coordinates
steps: POST /api/v1/check-in as Student B with consumed tokenId at TestClock T + 20 s
Assert HTTP 400 with outcome TokenAlreadyUsed (not ExpiredQr)
Assert errorCode TokenAlreadyUsed per BR-11 condition: token Consumed while still within 30-second window
Verify Student B attendance remains Pending
Verify TokenReuseAlert security audit entry created for reuse attempt per AC-10c
cases.expected: BR-11 applies when token state is Consumed regardless of remaining TTL; distinct from BR-03 ExpiredQr path for expired-but-not-consumed tokens

cases.title: Instructor denied POST /check-in with Consumed token — BR-11 not bypassed via staff role
preconditions: Authenticated Instructor session available
Active session with Consumed QrToken within validity window
Instructor role lacks checkin:submit per roles-permissions §2.2
steps: POST /api/v1/check-in with consumed tokenId and in-radius coordinates using Instructor session cookie
Assert HTTP status 403 Forbidden and errorCode Forbidden
Verify no Present attendance created for instructor userId
Verify no TokenReuseAlert or successful token consumption audit for instructor
Assert permission guard runs at validation-rules §5.1 step 3 before BR-11 token consumption evaluation at step 8
cases.expected: Only Student role triggers BR-11 check-in pipeline; staff accounts cannot consume or reuse QR tokens preventing proxy attendance

cases.title: Mobile check-in shows token reuse rejection at preflight when scanning shared Consumed QR
preconditions: Student B authenticated on mobile viewport 375×812
QrToken already Consumed by Student A within current 30-second window
Deep link or scanned QR contains consumed tokenId for Active session
steps: Student B opens /check-in?token=:consumedTokenId&session=:sessionId
Assert validating_token state per ui-states §4.1: inline spinner on scan step during GET /api/v1/check-in/tokens/:consumedTokenId/preflight
Verify preflight returns HTTP 403 with errorCode TokenAlreadyUsed and message Mã QR đã được sử dụng per API design §6.2
Assert data-testid gps-capture-step is absent; GpsCaptureStep never mounts after preflight failure per BR-15 and event-specific §2.3
Verify error outcome displayed on scan step (outcome state, not gps_capture)
Verify no success confirmation or Present badge shown
Verify recovery guidance to scan fresh QR from projector
cases.expected: BR-11 anti-proxy token sharing rejection visible at preflight on mobile web UI; scan step does not mount gps-capture-step until preflight passes; student directed to obtain new rotating QR

cases.title: Instructor session monitor may surface code-sharing alert after token reuse attempt
preconditions: Active session with instructor viewing live attendance monitor at /sessions/:sessionId/monitor
Student A checked in successfully consuming token T1
Student B attempted check-in with same consumed token T1 (TokenReuseAlert logged per BR-11)
steps: As Instructor, open session monitor page for Active session
Trigger Student B token reuse attempt via API or second mobile browser
Poll or wait for monitor refresh (≤5 s per FR-15 polling interval)
Verify code-sharing or token-reuse alert indicator visible on instructor monitor per BR-11 instructor alert
Verify Student B remains Pending in roster grid
Verify Student A remains Present in roster grid
cases.expected: BR-11 TokenReuseAlert security audit for token reuse may surface instructor-facing code-sharing alert during Active session monitor

cases.title: QrTokenRepository findByIdForUpdate prevents double consumption under concurrent Success attempts
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL test database is running
Active session with Valid qr_tokens row T1
QrTokenRepository.findByIdForUpdate wired per domain model §6
Two enrolled students with Pending attendance
steps: Simulate two concurrent repository transactions both calling findByIdForUpdate on T1
First transaction completes CheckInService Success path setting status Consumed
Second transaction attempts consumption on same tokenId
Assert second transaction receives TokenAlreadyUsed or blocks until first commits then rejects
Query qr_tokens for T1 and assert exactly one Consumed state with single consumedByStudentId
cases.expected: BR-11 at-most-one-consumption enforced at persistence layer via row-level lock on qr_tokens per domain model §5.2

cases.title: TokenAlreadyUsed response distinct from DuplicateCheckIn when different students share consumed token
preconditions: Student A Present from prior successful check-in consuming token T1
Student B authenticated with Pending attendance
Token T1 status Consumed within validity window
steps: Student B POST /api/v1/check-in with tokenId T1 and assert HTTP 400 TokenAlreadyUsed
Student A POST /api/v1/check-in with fresh Valid tokenId T2 and assert HTTP 409 DuplicateCheckIn
Compare response envelopes: TokenAlreadyUsed uses 400; DuplicateCheckIn uses 409
Assert distinct errorCode and Vietnamese messages per error-handling §4.4
Verify both failures create check_in_attempts rows with correct outcomes
cases.expected: BR-11 TokenAlreadyUsed (400) contract is distinguishable from BR-04 DuplicateCheckIn (409) for correct client handling of token-sharing vs duplicate-student scenarios

cases.title: Third sequential reuse attempt on same Consumed token creates additional TokenReuseAlert without state change
preconditions: QrToken T1 Consumed by Student A
Student B and Student C enrolled with Pending attendance
TestClock within 30-second validity window
steps: Student B CheckInService.submit with T1 and assert TokenAlreadyUsed
Student C CheckInService.submit with T1 and assert TokenAlreadyUsed
Query TokenReuseAlert security audit entries and assert two distinct entries for Student B and Student C per AC-10c
Query qr_tokens for T1 and assert status remains Consumed with consumedByStudentId Student A
Assert no Present records created for Student B or Student C
cases.expected: BR-11 has no exceptions: each token supports at most one successful consumption; every reuse attempt rejected and logged as append-only TokenReuseAlert

cases.title: Preflight rejects Consumed token with 403 TokenAlreadyUsed and TokenReuseAlert before GPS step
preconditions: Real PostgreSQL with Active session and qr_tokens row status Consumed by Student A
Token still within 30-second validity window (TestClock fixed before expiry)
Authenticated enrolled Student B with Pending attendance
steps: Invoke GET /api/v1/check-in/tokens/:consumedTokenId/preflight as Student B
Assert HTTP status 403 Forbidden
Assert Content-Type application/json; charset=utf-8
Assert response errorCode TokenAlreadyUsed and message Mã QR đã được sử dụng per API design §6.2
Verify TokenReuseAlert security audit entry contains sessionId, tokenId, and submitting Student B id per BR-11 outcome and AC-10c
Query attendance_records and qr_tokens and assert no writes occurred (read-only preflight per validation-rules §3.6a)
cases.expected: BR-11 enforced at preflight layer: 403 TokenAlreadyUsed and TokenReuseAlert security audit; client must not enter GPS capture per BR-15
